title: गायत्री चालीसा
devanagariText:
॥ दोहा ॥
जय गायत्री माता जय, वेद माता परम ज्ञान।
चालीसा तव गाइये, करहु कृपा भगवान॥
हृदय कमल में बैठि के, करो सकल कल्यान।
गायत्री माता तिहारो, कहूँ चालीसा गान॥

॥ चौपाई ॥
जय जय जय गायत्री माता।
तुम हो वेदों की विधाता॥
ब्रह्मा जी की शक्ति पुकारी।
तुम हो सृष्टि की अधिकारी॥
सावित्री सन्ध्या रूप तिहारा।
तीनों सन्ध्या ध्यान तिहारा॥
प्रातः काल ब्रह्मरूप ध्यावें।
मध्याह्न में वैष्णवी पावें॥
सायं काल शिवानी कहावें।
त्रिकाल सन्ध्या सब सुख पावें॥
चौबीस अक्षर मंत्र तिहारा।
सब मन्त्रन में श्रेष्ठ अपारा॥
ॐ भूर्भुवः स्वः ततः सवितुः।
वरेण्यम् भर्गो देवस्य धीमहि॥
धियो यो नः प्रचोदयात्।
यही मंत्र सबसे परम ज्ञात॥
तीन लोक में शक्ति तिहारी।
तुम हो सकल विद्या अधिकारी॥
विश्वामित्र ने मंत्र दिया।
त्रिलोक में प्रकाश किया॥
द्विज मात्र को उपनयन संस्कार।
गायत्री मंत्र का अधिकार॥
जो नित संध्या वन्दन करे।
सो पापन से सदा तिरे॥
सूर्य देव की शक्ति पुकारी।
सविता तेज की अधिकारी॥
बुद्धि विवेक प्रदान करावे।
अज्ञान तिमिर को दूर भगावे॥
पंच मुखी गायत्री माता।
पंच प्राण की तुम हो ज्ञाता॥
पंच तत्व से जग रचा तुमने।
पंच देवता पूजें तुमने॥
हंस वाहिनी वेद पुकारे।
कमण्डलु पुस्तक हस्त तिहारे॥
श्वेत वसन मुख चन्द्र समाना।
सकल विद्या का तुम हो खजाना॥
पुष्कर तीर्थ तुम्हारा प्यारा।
ब्रह्मा संग विराजे न्यारा॥
गायत्री शक्तिपीठ विराजे।
सकल तीर्थ में मंगल साजे॥
जो गायत्री जप नित करे।
सो ब्रह्म ज्ञान को प्राप्त करे॥
सहस्र जप मनोरथ पूरे।
लाख जप ऋद्धि सिद्धि ना दूरे॥
पंडित विद्वान गायत्री ध्यावें।
ब्रह्म तेज मुख पर छा जावे॥
रोग शोक दारिद्रय मिटावे।
गायत्री मंत्र जो नित ध्यावे॥
सन्तान सुख प्रदान करावे।
गृहस्थ जीवन सुख से भरावे॥
मोक्ष मार्ग का द्वार खुलावे।
गायत्री माता भव पार लगावे॥
चारों वेद में शक्ति तिहारी।
गायत्री तुम जगत उजियारी॥
तुम्हरे बिना न यज्ञ सिधावे।
हवन होम में तुम्हें बुलावे॥
चालीसा गायत्री गाये।
सो सब सुख सम्पत्ति पाये॥
भक्ति भाव से पाठ जो करे।
सो संसार सागर से तरे॥
गायत्री माता की जय जय जय।
करो कृपा सब जन पर सदय॥

॥ दोहा ॥
गायत्री चालीसा पढ़े, करे सन्ध्या नित ध्यान।
बुद्धि विवेक प्रकाश हो, पावे ब्रह्म ज्ञान॥
transliteration:
|| Doha ||
Jai Gayatri Mata Jai, Ved Mata Param Gyan.
Chalisa Tav Gaiye, Karahu Kripa Bhagwan.
Hriday Kamal Mein Baithi Ke, Karo Sakal Kalyan.
Gayatri Mata Tiharo, Kahun Chalisa Gaan.

|| Chaupai ||
Jai Jai Jai Gayatri Mata.
Tum Ho Vedon Ki Vidhata.
Brahma Ji Ki Shakti Pukari.
Tum Ho Srishti Ki Adhikari.
Savitri Sandhya Roop Tihara.
Teenon Sandhya Dhyan Tihara.
Pratah Kal Brahmaroop Dhyave.
Madhyahn Mein Vaishnavi Pave.
Sayam Kal Shivani Kahave.
Trikal Sandhya Sab Sukh Pave.
Chaubis Akshar Mantra Tihara.
Sab Mantran Mein Shresht Apara.
Om Bhurbhuvah Swah Tatah Savituh.
Varenyam Bhargo Devasya Dhimahi.
Dhiyo Yo Nah Prachodayat.
Yahi Mantra Sabse Param Gyat.
Teen Lok Mein Shakti Tihari.
Tum Ho Sakal Vidya Adhikari.
Vishwamitra Ne Mantra Diya.
Trilok Mein Prakash Kiya.
Dwij Matra Ko Upnayan Sanskar.
Gayatri Mantra Ka Adhikar.
Jo Nit Sandhya Vandan Kare.
So Papan Se Sada Tire.
Surya Dev Ki Shakti Pukari.
Savita Tej Ki Adhikari.
Buddhi Vivek Pradan Karave.
Agyan Timir Ko Door Bhagave.
Panch Mukhi Gayatri Mata.
Panch Pran Ki Tum Ho Gyata.
Panch Tatva Se Jag Racha Tumne.
Panch Devta Pujen Tumne.
Hans Vahini Ved Pukare.
Kamandalu Pustak Hast Tihare.
Shwet Vasan Mukh Chandra Samana.
Sakal Vidya Ka Tum Ho Khajana.
Pushkar Tirth Tumhara Pyara.
Brahma Sang Viraje Nyara.
Gayatri Shaktipeeth Viraje.
Sakal Tirth Mein Mangal Saje.
Jo Gayatri Jap Nit Kare.
So Brahm Gyan Ko Prapt Kare.
Sahasr Jap Manorath Pure.
Lakh Jap Riddhi Siddhi Na Dure.
Pandit Vidwan Gayatri Dhyave.
Brahm Tej Mukh Par Chha Jave.
Rog Shok Daridrya Mitave.
Gayatri Mantra Jo Nit Dhyave.
Santan Sukh Pradan Karave.
Grihasth Jivan Sukh Se Bharave.
Moksh Marg Ka Dwar Khulave.
Gayatri Mata Bhav Par Lagave.
Charon Ved Mein Shakti Tihari.
Gayatri Tum Jagat Ujiyari.
Tumhre Bina Na Yagya Sidhave.
Havan Hom Mein Tumhe Bulave.
Chalisa Gayatri Gaye.
So Sab Sukh Sampatti Paye.
Bhakti Bhav Se Path Jo Kare.
So Sansar Sagar Se Tare.
Gayatri Mata Ki Jai Jai Jai.
Karo Kripa Sab Jan Par Saday.

|| Doha ||
Gayatri Chalisa Padhe, Kare Sandhya Nit Dhyan.
Buddhi Vivek Prakash Ho, Pave Brahm Gyan.
hindiMeaning:
जय गायत्री माता! वेद माता! परम ज्ञान! आपकी चालीसा गाता हूँ, कृपा करो भगवान! हृदय कमल में बैठकर सकल कल्याण करो।

आप वेदों की विधाता हैं, ब्रह्मा जी की शक्ति हैं, सृष्टि की अधिकारिणी हैं। सावित्री, सन्ध्या आपके रूप हैं। तीनों संध्या में आपका ध्यान किया जाता है।
benefits: Enhances intellect, wisdom, and spiritual knowledge
Purifies mind and grants clarity of thought
Essential for those who perform daily Sandhya Vandana
Traditionally recited for removal of sins and negative karma accumulated over lifetimes
Invokes the blessing of brahma tejas (divine radiance) on the devotee
description: The 40 chaupais give the Chalisa its name (chalis = forty); framing dohas bring the full recitation to 43 verse-units. Among the most cherished prayers to Goddess Gayatri, Gayatri Chalisa (गायत्री चालीसा) stands as a sacred 40-verse devotional hymn. Rooted in Traditional Chalisa Sangrah, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Regular recitation is believed to enhance intellect, wisdom, and spiritual knowledge, bestow Brahma Tejas (divine radiance) on the devotee, and remove sins and negative karma accumulated over lifetimes. This prayer is traditionally recited on Sunday (Ravivar) and Thursday (Guruvar) and during Gayatri Jayanti to invoke divine grace. Comprising 42 verses, it can be completed in approximately 15 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Gayatri Chalisa in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Gayatri Chalisa in Hindi with English transliteration and meaning. Prayer to Gayatri Mata, the Veda Mata, for wisdom and spiritual growth.

seoFaqs.question: How does the Gayatri Chalisa relate to the Gayatri Mantra?
seoFaqs.answer: The Gayatri Mantra (Rigveda 3.62.10, beginning Om bhur bhuvah svah, tat savitur varenyam) is a twenty-four syllable Vedic chant addressed to Savitr, the solar source of illumination, asking that our intellect be inspired. The Gayatri Chalisa is a forty-verse devotional hymn in vernacular Hindi that personifies that mantra as the Goddess Gayatri, mother of the Vedas. The chalisa is therefore not a substitute for the mantra but a bhakti complement: the mantra is the essential Vedic seed, and the chalisa is the loving narrative praise of the deity who is its living form. Many devotees chant the mantra silently as japa and recite the chalisa aloud as stotra-paath.

seoFaqs.question: Why is Gayatri called Veda Mata, the mother of the Vedas?
seoFaqs.answer: Gayatri is honoured as Veda Mata because her twenty-four-syllable mantra is considered the seed from which all Vedic knowledge unfolds. The chalisa develops this metaphor by describing her as five-faced, with each face symbolising one of the five pranas, the five elements, and the five sheaths (koshas) of the human being, while her ten arms hold weapons and boons that represent the powers of dharma. Reciting the chalisa is understood as approaching the entire Vedic corpus through its presiding mother, much as a devotee approaches a vast library through the librarian who knows where every text resides.

seoFaqs.question: When is the most auspicious time to chant Gayatri Chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: The traditional times are the three sandhyas: dawn, noon and dusk, which together make up the daily sandhya vandana practised by many Hindus. Of these, brahma muhurta (roughly ninety minutes before sunrise) is considered the most powerful, when the mind is naturally still. The chalisa is also recited on Gayatri Jayanti (Jyeshtha Shukla Ekadashi), on Vasant Panchami, on every Friday for the Devi, and on personal sankalpa days such as the start of an academic year, before exams, or during periods of mental fatigue. Members of the Gayatri Parivar tradition founded by Pandit Shriram Sharma Acharya in Shantikunj recite both the mantra and the chalisa as part of a structured daily sadhana.

seoFaqs.question: Are there eligibility restrictions for chanting the Gayatri Chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: The classical Vedic Gayatri Mantra was historically restricted to those who had received upanayana (sacred-thread initiation), and that question has produced centuries of debate. The Gayatri Chalisa, however, is a vernacular stotra rather than a Vedic mantra and is universally treated as open to all devotees regardless of caste, gender or initiation status. Reformist movements such as the Gayatri Parivar have actively encouraged women, children and people of every background to chant both the mantra and the chalisa. The shared emphasis is on cleanliness, attentiveness, and bhava rather than on ritual eligibility.

seoFaqs.question: What benefits do practitioners traditionally associate with Gayatri Chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional sources describe the chalisa as cultivating buddhi (intellect), medha (memory), tejas (radiance) and viveka (discernment). Students chant it for clarity in study, professionals for steady focus at work, and seekers for purification of the mind that supports meditation. The chalisa's closing verses promise removal of obstacles, freedom from anxiety, and the steady inner light symbolised by Savitr the sun. Devotees often pair recitation with simple disciplines such as truthful speech, vegetarian diet on the day of chanting, and offering arghya (water libation) to the rising sun, treating these together as a complete Gayatri sadhana.

seoFaqs.question: How is the Gayatri Chalisa structured across its forty verses?
seoFaqs.answer: Like other chalisas in the Goswami Tulsidas tradition, the Gayatri Chalisa opens with a doha (couplet) of invocation, moves into forty chaupais (quatrains), and closes with a phala-shruti doha that lists the fruits of recitation. The opening verses praise Gayatri as the mother of the Vedas and consort of Brahma. The middle verses describe her five faces, ten arms, and her position as the source of Saraswati, Lakshmi and Durga energies. Later verses recall episodes where her grace removed the suffering of devotees and rishis. The closing verses promise that sincere recitation, especially at the three sandhyas, brings illumined intellect and steady prosperity in this world and the next.
